sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: INVESTOR ALERT: Pomerantz Law Firm Reminds Investors with Losses on their Investment in Aquestive Therapeutics, Inc. of Class Action Lawsuit and Upcoming Deadlines – AQST
feed.summary: The Pomerantz LLP law firm has announced a class action lawsuit against Aquestive Therapeutics, Inc. (AQST) regarding alleged securities fraud or unlawful business practices. The lawsuit follows Aquestive's stock price drop after the FDA identified deficiencies in its New Drug Application for Anaphylm, delaying its approval indefinitely. Investors who purchased Aquestive securities during the Class Period have until May 4, 2026, to seek appointment as Lead Plaintiff.

feed.title: ADT Inc. (NYSE:ADT) Q4 2025 earnings call transcript
feed.summary: This article is an earnings call transcript for ADT Inc. (NYSE:ADT) for the fourth quarter of 2025. It details the discussions, financial results, and management commentary from the company's Q4 2025 earnings call.

feed.title: Guarantor: JPMorgan Chase & Co.
feed.summary: JPMorgan Chase Financial Company LLC has issued $2,000,000 in Auto Callable Buffered Return Enhanced Notes linked to the MSCI Emerging Markets Index, due March 28, 2030, and fully and unconditionally guaranteed by JPMorgan Chase & Co. These notes are designed for investors seeking an early exit at a premium if the index meets a Call Value on the Review Date, or an uncapped return of 1.40 times any appreciation of the Index at maturity. Investing in these notes carries significant risks, including the potential loss of principal and credit risks of both JPMorgan Financial and JPMorgan Chase & Co.

feed.title: Georgia Power’s $912M storm damage debt could soon be on your bill
feed.summary: Georgia Power is seeking regulatory approval to recover $912 million in storm damage costs, primarily due to Hurricane Helene. The company proposes to increase customer bills by 2.2%, or an average of $4.42 per month for typical users, to fund increased storm damage reserves. Critics argue this forces Georgians to pay twice, once through bills and again through more severe storms fueled by climate change, while the company plans to use a portion of federal tax credits to offset the debt.

feed.title: Bunge shares slip after short seller calls for accounting probe
feed.summary: Bunge shares experienced a slip following a call from activist investor and short seller The Friendly Bear for an accounting probe into the agribusiness giant. The short seller alleged that Bunge's financial results were "misleadingly inflated" by overstating mark-to-market valuations in its structured transactions. Bunge has yet to respond to these specific allegations.

feed.title: Morgan Stanley Finds Less Treasuries Liquidity on War Volatility
feed.summary: Morgan Stanley strategists report a significant drop in Treasury market liquidity, particularly in short-term notes, following war-related volatility and forced selling. This decline is evident in trade data from BrokerTec Inc. since the US attack on Iran on February 28, with longer-maturity tenors showing more stability. The reduced liquidity has been characterized by soaring yields on two-year notes as traders adjusted expectations for Federal Reserve interest-rate policy.

feed.title: People on the move: NRG taps generation veteran as next CEO
feed.summary: NRG Energy has appointed Robert J. Gaudette as its next CEO, succeeding Lawrence Coben, effective April 30. Other significant appointments include Justin Johnson becoming CEO of Arevon Energy, Ingmar Sterzing joining Zachry Group as president of nuclear construction, and Heath Lovell being promoted to COO at Hallador Energy. Additionally, Patrick Martino has transitioned to site vice president for Vogtle Units 1&2 at Southern Nuclear.

feed.title: Copper Miner ETF (CPER) Hits New 52-Week High
feed.summary: The United States Copper Index Fund (CPER) has reached a new 52-week high, surging 29% from its low. This performance is attributed to increased copper prices driven by expected demand, Chinese stimulus measures, and supply-chain disruptions. While the ETF holds a Zacks #3 Rank (Hold), its weighted alpha of 30.64 and 20-day volatility of 25.5% suggest potential for risk-aggressive investors.

feed.title: Medtronic lowers earnings forecast after MiniMed IPO
feed.summary: Medtronic has lowered its earnings forecast for fiscal year 2026 due to the initial public offering of its diabetes spinoff, MiniMed, and the earlier-than-anticipated clearance of MiniMed's Flex insulin pump. The revised outlook includes a $157 million charge MiniMed will pay related to future payments to Blackstone, which funded the pump's development, as well as an expected decrease in earnings per share due to the IPO's timing and the pump's FDA clearance. Medtronic now anticipates adjusted earnings per share of $5.50 to $5.54, a 12-cent reduction from its previous forecast.

feed.title: NetApp seals Elastio and Commvault deals for more resilience and less ransomware
feed.summary: NetApp has partnered with Elastio to enhance ransomware detection in its ONTAP snapshots and with Commvault to improve rapid recovery from attacks. The collaboration aims to provide layered protection against cyber threats, utilizing Elastio's Deep File Inspection and Commvault's recovery capabilities with NetApp's AI-driven ransomware detection. These initiatives are designed to minimize data loss, accelerate recovery, and ensure business resilience across on-premises and cloud environments.

feed.title: Catastrophe losses plunge, driving US net underwriting gain: Verisk
feed.summary: The U.S. insurance industry saw an estimated net underwriting gain of $63 billion in 2025, more than double 2024's figure, according to a report by Verisk and the American Property Casualty Insurance Association. This significant increase was primarily due to unusually low catastrophe losses, including a nearly 90% decline in hurricane-related claims, leading to a near-record low combined ratio of 92.9%. Despite a rise in net written premiums, net income after taxes declined due to a decrease in net investment gains.

feed.title: Alcoa Corp. Faces Weak Start with 11.48% Gap Down Amid Market Concerns
feed.summary: Alcoa Corp. opened with a significant 11.48% gap down, underperforming the S&P 500, and has also lagged the index over the past month. Despite the challenging trading day, the company exhibits a conservative leverage approach with a low debt-to-equity ratio of 0.13 and a strong return on equity at 29.96%. Its market capitalization is approximately USD 17.57 billion, and its P/E ratio stands at 10.00.

feed.title: West Virginia American Water continues to invest in infrastructure with $560,000 project in Dunbar
feed.summary: West Virginia American Water is investing $560,000 in a project to replace 720 feet of aging pipe on 18th Street in Dunbar, as part of a larger plan to

feed.title: ServiceNow (NOW) Sold Off on AI Fears but Long-Term Competitive Edge Remains Intact
feed.summary: Montaka Global Investments' Q4 2025 investor letter discusses ServiceNow (NYSE: NOW), highlighting that the stock's sell-off due to AI disruption fears is likely temporary. The investment firm believes ServiceNow's large-scale, mission-critical software systems are difficult to recreate and that its competitive advantages in R&D, distribution, and data will allow it to extract value from AI. Despite the stock's decline, Montaka Global Investments views ServiceNow as significantly undervalued, ranking it among the most popular stocks held by hedge funds.

feed.title: Western Digital Riding on AI & Cloud Boom to Power Growth
feed.summary: Western Digital Corporation (WDC) is poised for growth due to the increasing adoption of AI and cloud technologies, which are driving demand for high-density storage. The company is innovating with high-capacity drives, including ePMR and HAMR technologies, and expects strong momentum in data center demand. Competitors like Seagate Technology Holdings (STX) and Everpure, Inc. (PSTG) are also benefiting from these trends, with WDC showing a strong Zacks Rank #1 (Strong Buy).

feed.title: MGM Resorts launches all-inclusive package for Luxor, Excalibur
feed.summary: MGM Resorts has introduced a new all-inclusive vacation package for its Luxor and Excalibur hotels in Las Vegas. Starting at $330 plus tax for a two-night stay for two guests, the bundle includes hotel, dining, entertainment, parking, and resort fees. Travel for these packages begins on April 6, with daily meals, two show tickets, and a Big Apple Coaster ride included.

feed.title: From failure to hit to a $6.7 billion buyout: Terns Pharma gets big offer from Merck
feed.summary: Terns Pharmaceuticals, led by Amy Burroughs, is set to be acquired by Merck for $6.7 billion. This significant offer comes after the company recently delivered encouraging early data for its oral drug in December. The acquisition marks a major milestone for Terns Pharma.

feed.title: Herdez Refrigerated Entrées add convenience to traditional meats
feed.summary: Hormel Foods has introduced Herdez Refrigerated Entrées, offering consumers a convenient way to enjoy traditional, authentic Mexican flavors that typically require extensive preparation. These entrées cater to a growing demand for both convenience and traditional foods, with Herdez leveraging its strong brand recognition as the number one salsa brand in Mexico. The product line aims to provide familiar, satisfying flavors in a quick and easy-to-prepare format, complementing other Herdez offerings like taqueria sauces and salsas.

feed.title: Can High-NA EUV Adoption Accelerate ASML's Long-Term Revenue Growth?
feed.summary: ASML Holdings' High-NA EUV technology is transitioning from development to early adoption, with Intel already accepting its EXE:5200 tool for high-volume manufacturing. This move, coupled with increasing AI demand, is boosting the need for advanced lithography tools as customers shift to smaller nanometer nodes. While High-NA EUV is crucial for ASML's long-term revenue growth towards its 2030 targets, its broader EUV business remains the primary growth driver in the short term.

feed.title: ATI vs. CRS: Which Specialty Metals Stock Should You Invest in Now?
feed.summary: This article compares ATI Inc. (ATI) and Carpenter Technology Corporation (CRS), two specialty metals companies benefiting from strong demand in aerospace and defense. Both companies exhibit robust growth prospects and are improving profitability through product mix and operational efficiency. The analysis ultimately suggests ATI might have a slight edge due to its more attractive valuation and broader market exposure.

feed.title: AMC Entertainment Holdings stock hits new 52-week low amid CEO controversy and class action lawsuit
feed.summary: AMC Entertainment Holdings stock has fallen to a new 52-week low of $1.00 on the NYSE, underperforming broader markets. This decline follows CEO Adam Aron's dismissive response to critics using a Taylor Swift reference, the filing of a new class action lawsuit, and a share registration filing that suggests further dilution risk for investors. The article highlights the ongoing challenges AMC faces, including sector headwinds, persistent losses, and critical investor sentiment.

feed.title: Is Fifth Third (FITB) Quietly Reframing Its Franchise Around Community Impact And Digital Engagement?
feed.summary: Fifth Third Bancorp is expanding its community and economic development efforts through initiatives like the "Small Towns & Small Cities" program in Lima, Ohio, while simultaneously enhancing its digital banking tools. This dual strategy aims to integrate social impact with digital engagement, strengthening the bank's franchise and brand amidst fintech competition and potential loan growth challenges. The company's investment narrative emphasizes digital capabilities, disciplined credit, and community-focused lending, with revenue projected to reach $10.4 billion and earnings $2.6 billion by 2028.

feed.title: Tapestry’s Gen Z Customer Surge Might Change The Case For Investing In Tapestry (TPR)
feed.summary: Tapestry (TPR) recently highlighted its success in attracting 3.7 million new customers, particularly from Gen Z, at CERAWeek 2026. This customer growth is translating into strong financial performance, with Q2 sales of US$2,502.4 million and net income of US$561.3 million, supporting its investment narrative. However, the company still faces potential risks from rising tariffs and shifting trade policies, which could impact margins despite the positive customer acquisition trends.

feed.title: Digital Realty’s Milan Expansion Might Change The Case For Investing In Digital Realty Trust (DLR)
feed.summary: Digital Realty (DLR) recently expanded into the Milan market by acquiring land for a new data center campus, making it the only global data center platform with a presence in Italy's two main connectivity hubs. This move aims to support cloud and AI services, reinforcing its Mediterranean infrastructure. While this expansion supports the company's growth narrative, investors should also consider its 2026 guidance, which includes revenue projections between US$6.6 billion and US$6.7 billion, and diluted EPS between US$2.55 and US$2.65, alongside potential financial needs and interest rate volatility.

feed.title: Applied Materials Gains Spotlight In Chip Supply Chain Build Out
feed.summary: Applied Materials is positioned as a key beneficiary of new initiatives aimed at strengthening semiconductor supply chains, including a U.S. allied investment program and a partnership with UPS in Taiwan. The company's stock has seen significant growth, reflecting investor interest in its role in chip manufacturing. These developments underscore Applied Materials' operational importance and potential for further growth within the semiconductor industry.

feed.title: Levi & Korsinsky Notifies Gartner, Inc. Investors of a Class Act
feed.summary: Levi & Korsinsky, LLP has announced a class action securities lawsuit against Gartner, Inc. (NYSE: IT). The lawsuit alleges that Gartner made materially false and misleading statements regarding its growth rates and ability to meet consulting revenue targets and maintain its contract value growth rate between February 4, 2025, and February 2, 2026. Investors who suffered losses during this period have until May 18, 2026, to request to be appointed as lead plaintiff.

feed.title: Oracle applications chief sees enterprise AI agents as task-specific helpers
feed.summary: During the Oracle AI Summit in London, Steve Miranda, Oracle's executive vice-president of applications development, discussed the company's new Fusion Agentic Applications. These applications leverage AI agents for task-specific operations across various enterprise functions like ERP and HCM, with an emphasis on human oversight to prevent incorrect outcomes. Oracle plans to evolve its pricing model from user-based subscriptions to include transaction-based or company-size pricing for AI functionalities.

feed.title: Illinois Tool Works stock faces mixed institutional flows amid strong FY2026 EPS guidance and dividend announcement
feed.summary: Illinois Tool Works (ITW) stock opened at $265.12 with mixed institutional interest, as Pensionfund Sabic increased its stake while E. Ohman J or Asset Management reduced theirs. The company's FY2026 EPS guidance of $11.00-$11.40 surpasses consensus, making it attractive for US income investors, especially with a 2.43% annualized dividend yield ahead of the March 31 ex-dividend date. Despite some institutional trims, ownership remains high, signaling ongoing interest in ITW's resilient margin expansion and defensive growth in the cyclical industrial sector.

feed.title: Why Campbell’s $1B Rao’s brand is winning on sauce
feed.summary: Campbell’s Rao’s Homemade brand, acquired in 2024, has achieved $1 billion in annual sales, yet continues aggressive growth strategies by emphasizing its premium ingredients and encouraging consumer trial. Despite its higher price point, the company believes that demonstrating the value of its quality ingredients, such as tomatoes from Mt. Vesuvius, will continue to drive household penetration and sales, even as it expands into new creamy sauce categories.

feed.title: Thermo Fisher Scientific and SHL Medical Collaborate to Deliver Fully Integrated Drug-Device Solutio
feed.summary: Thermo Fisher Scientific and SHL Medical have announced a strategic collaboration to provide fully integrated drug-device solutions. This partnership, combined with an expansion at Thermo Fisher's Ridgefield, NJ site, will offer sterile manufacturing, device assembly, and commercial packaging for drug-device combination products in a single U.S. location. The initiative aims to streamline supply chains and accelerate the delivery of self-administered injectable therapies to patients.

feed.title: Freeport and Newmont Get a Boost. Why Mining Stocks are Rising.
feed.summary: Mining companies Freeport and Newmont saw their stock prices rally on Wednesday. This surge is attributed to rising precious metal prices, following a report in The Wall Street Journal about a U.S.-drafted peace plan concerning the ongoing conflict with Iran.

feed.title: Wall Street Analysts Predict a 25.39% Upside in Jack Henry (JKHY): Here's What You Should Know
feed.summary: Wall Street analysts project a 25.4% upside for Jack Henry (JKHY), with a mean price target of $199.21. Despite this, investors are cautioned against relying solely on price targets due to their potential for bias. However, increasing optimism among analysts regarding the company's earnings prospects and a Zacks Rank #2 (Buy) suggest a potential upside for the stock.

feed.title: JPMorgan Chase & Co. Has Lowered Expectations for Travere Therapeutics (NASDAQ:TVTX) Stock Price
feed.summary: JPMorgan Chase & Co. has reduced its price target for Travere Therapeutics (NASDAQ:TVTX) from $44.00 to $41.00 but maintained an "overweight" rating, indicating a potential upside of 53.56% from the previous close. The consensus among analysts is a "Moderate Buy" with an average target price of $38.38. Despite recent insider selling, major institutions like Vanguard and State Street have increased their stakes in the biopharmaceutical company.

feed.title: Buffett Vs. Ackman: One Dumped Amazon Stock, The Other Bought More — Here's Who Came Out Ahead
feed.summary: Billionaire investors Warren Buffett and Bill Ackman took opposing stances on Amazon.com (NASDAQ:AMZN) stock in the fourth quarter of 2025, with Buffett significantly trimming his stake and Ackman increasing his. While Ackman's additional Amazon shares have since dropped, Buffett's timely sale appears to have saved his investors from further losses as Magnificent Seven stocks have struggled year-to-date in 2026. This article details their differing investment decisions on Amazon and other major tech companies like Alphabet (NASDAQ:GOOGL) and Meta Platforms (NASDAQ:META).

feed.title: Oppenheimer Raises Aramark (NYSE:ARMK) Price Target to $50.00
feed.summary: Oppenheimer has increased its price target for Aramark (NYSE:ARMK) from $43.00 to $50.00, reiterating an "outperform" rating and implying a potential 20.91% upside. This adjustment reflects broader analyst positivity, with eight "Buy" and two "Hold" ratings and an average target of $48.56, especially after Aramark's recent slight EPS beat, 6.1% year-over-year revenue growth, and optimistic FY2026 guidance. The company's stock is rated as a "Moderate Buy" based on these analyst recommendations.

feed.title: Teamsters terminate agreement, strike at Smurfit Westrock site set for closure
feed.summary: Teamsters members initiated a strike at a Smurfit Westrock corrugated converting plant in Wakefield, Massachusetts, demanding fair wages, affordable healthcare, and no cuts to retirement contributions. This action follows Smurfit Westrock's announcement to close the facility and lay off 91 employees as part of its ongoing optimization plan. The company claims the union terminated their labor agreement, while the Teamsters insist Smurfit Westrock should return to the bargaining table.

feed.title: What is behind Moderna stock's recent gain in value today
feed.summary: Moderna (MRNA) stock surged 4.65% to $53.73 today, driven by a $2.25 billion settlement with Arbutus Biopharma and Genevant, resolving lipid nanoparticle patent disputes. The company also boasts strong cash reserves and ongoing technical improvements to its Spikevax vaccine. Experts offer mixed views on the stock's future, with some highlighting strong bullish signals and others advising caution due to overbought indicators and the significant settlement liability.

feed.title: AerCap Holdings NV stock faces aircraft leasing challenges amid global aviation recovery pressures
feed.summary: AerCap Holdings NV is navigating the complex aircraft leasing market, influenced by airline fleet modernization and supply chain issues. The company maintains a robust portfolio and benefits from sustained air travel growth, particularly in international routes. While facing risks from potential airline insolvencies and geopolitical tensions, AerCap's strategic initiatives and market leadership offer a resilient investment opportunity for US investors.

feed.title: Thermo Fisher Scientific and SHL Medical Collaborate to Deliver Fully Integrated Drug-Device Solutions
feed.summary: Thermo Fisher Scientific has announced a strategic collaboration with SHL Medical and an expansion of its Ridgefield, NJ facility. This partnership aims to provide fully integrated drug-device solutions in the U.S., combining sterile fill-finish, device final assembly, and commercial packaging for drug-device combination products. The initiative seeks to streamline supply chains and accelerate the delivery of therapies, especially for self-administered injectable treatments.

feed.title: AptarGroup Inc stock faces CEO transition in 2026 amid analyst upgrades and pharma delivery wins
feed.summary: AptarGroup Inc. is undergoing a CEO transition in September 2026, with Gael Touya appointed to succeed the current leader. The company is receiving analyst upgrades due to its strong balance sheet, defensive pharmaceutical exposure, and new collaborations in nasal drug delivery systems. Despite a recent stock decline, these strategic moves and financial health position AptarGroup as a resilient option for US investors navigating market volatility.

feed.title: Arrow Electronics Inc stock faces valuation scrutiny amid supply chain shifts and institutional buyi
feed.summary: Arrow Electronics Inc has garnered renewed attention from institutional investors like Gradient Investments LLC, which acquired 6,944 shares, highlighting confidence in the company's role in the evolving electronics supply chain. The company benefits from its position in high-growth areas such as AI and 5G, providing value-added services and leveraging its global footprint to navigate geopolitical tensions. Its financial resilience and exposure to US policy tailwinds further strengthen its appeal to investors, though risks like inventory overhang and competition remain.

feed.title: Anavex Life Sciences Corp Faces Competitive Pressure Amid Financial Year Results
feed.summary: Anavex Life Sciences Corp. (NASDAQ: AVXL) has seen its stock drop by over 30% following unimpressive clinical trial results and an operating loss of $6.78M due to high R&D expenses. Despite financial volatility and investor wariness, the company maintains a strong cash position of $131.75M and a zero-debt-to-equity ratio, indicating financial resilience. The market's reaction hinges on whether Anavex's ongoing research investments will translate into significant breakthroughs and future financial success.

feed.title: Target Corp stock faces pressure amid shifting retail dynamics and economic headwinds in 2026
feed.summary: Target Corp (TGT) is facing significant pressure in 2026 due to shifting consumer spending patterns, inventory adjustments, and intense competition from e-commerce giants. The retailer is experiencing softer traffic and increased reliance on promotions, impacting gross margins. Investors are closely monitoring Target's ability to navigate these challenges by balancing inventory, pricing strategies, and digital sales growth while adapting to budget-conscious shoppers in a high-interest-rate environment.

feed.title: Avino Silver & Gold Mines (TSX:ASM) Joins S&P/TSX Index Is Its Risk Profile Quietly Shifting?
feed.summary: Avino Silver & Gold Mines (TSX:ASM) has been included in the S&P/TSX Capped Composite Index in March 2026. This inclusion is expected to increase the company's visibility, institutional demand, and trading volumes, potentially shifting its investment narrative. While this boosts liquidity, the core focus remains on operational execution, disciplined capital allocation, and meeting 2026 production and cost guidance, with investors still needing to consider cost structure and project spend risks against silver price fluctuations.

feed.title: Latest WHG News - Westwood Announces Monthly Income Distributi...
feed.summary: This page provides a comprehensive news feed for Westwood Holdings Group (WHG), an investment management and wealth management firm. It covers various aspects of the company's news including quarterly earnings, assets under management, energy and real assets activities, ETF announcements, and strategic partnerships. Recent updates include monthly income distributions for several ETFs, the surpassing of $250 million in ETF platform assets, the liquidation of a global equity ETF, and Q4 and full-year 2025 financial results.

feed.title: Arm Leaps Ahead: Strategic Moves Boost Stock as New CPU Revolutionizes AI Centers
feed.summary: Arm Holdings plc's stock has surged by 15.54% following strategic growth initiatives, including the debut of its AGI CPU for AI data centers and strong partnerships with companies like Meta. HSBC has upgraded Arm to 'Buy' with a doubled price target of $205, reflecting confidence in its transition from smartphones to AI CPUs. The company anticipates $15 billion in revenue from its chip division within five years, despite a high P/E ratio indicating ambitious investor expectations.

feed.title: agilon health inc stock faces pressure from 7.6% drop amid cost concerns and looming 1-for-10 reverse split
feed.summary: Agilon Health Inc. shares recently dropped 7.6% due to increasing cost pressures in its Medicare-centric model, alongside the announcement of a 1-for-10 reverse stock split effective March 31, 2026, aimed at improving liquidity. The company faces investor skepticism as year-to-date losses reach 11.8%, with shares trading near 52-week lows, compounded by ongoing lawsuits alleging misleading 2025 financial guidance. This situation highlights the challenges of balancing platform scalability with capitated risk execution in the volatile healthcare sector.

feed.title: TYL SEC Filings - Tyler Technologies 10-K, 10-Q, 8-K Forms
feed.summary: This page provides a comprehensive resource for Tyler Technologies (TYL) SEC filings, including annual 10-K reports, quarterly 10-Q earnings, 8-K material events, and insider trading forms. It details recent insider stock transactions and offers AI-powered summaries to help investors understand complex regulatory disclosures. The site also includes market data for TYL, such as current stock price and market capitalization.

feed.title: Amcor plc stock positioned strongly in booming connected packaging market amid sustainability push
feed.summary: Amcor plc is strategically positioned in the rapidly growing connected packaging market, integrating smart technologies like QR codes and NFC into recyclable formats. The company's focus on traceability, consumer engagement, and sustainability is driving its competitive edge, especially in key growth regions like North America, and attracting US investors. Despite risks such as intense competition and regulatory changes, Amcor's innovation and diversified portfolio present a strong play in the evolving packaging sector.

feed.title: Rail merger ‘not good for our industry,’ says BNSF CEO
feed.summary: BNSF Railway CEO Katie Farmer has expressed strong opposition to the proposed merger between Union Pacific and Norfolk Southern, stating it would be detrimental to the rail industry by reducing options, raising rates, and failing to improve efficiencies. She highlighted that historical data contradicts UP's growth projections and questioned how an $85 billion merger with a premium would be paid for if growth didn't materialize, potentially burdening shippers. Farmer also noted significant opposition from customers and congressional members, emphasizing that the merger, if approved, would be the first reviewed under new, more stringent public interest and competition enhancement rules.
